bio.fun_fact: Wears a special necklace with a lightning bolt on it for every race
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Speciality: 500m, Team Sprint, 1,000m
  • Played college soccer as a defender at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
  • Wears a special necklace with a lightning bolt on it for every race
  • Father and brother both played Division III football at Occidental and the University of Chicago, respectively
Biographical Information 

Sarah Warren grew up in Willowbrook, Illinois, as a standout in speedskating and soccer. In speedskating, a sport she started at age 13, she competed at age 15 in short track at the Youth Winter Olympic Games Innsbruck 2012, finishing fourth in the 500m. In addition to heading three junior world championships in speedskating, Warren played college soccer at Illinois.

Warren struggled with injuries throughout her career. She tore her ACL twice and underwent four surgeries while playing college soccer.  Warren made her first U.S. national team in 2022 and made her world championships debut in 2024, winning a silver medal with teammates Erin Jackson and Brittany Bowe in a team sprint, an event not currently on the Olympic program.

Off the ice, Warren graduated from the University of Illinois in 2019 with a major in bioengineering and a minor in chemistry. She is currently pursuing a master's in biomedical engineering from Johns Hopkins University.

World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2024 – silver (Team Sprint - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team Sprint - Women 2024
  • Medals: 1 (1 silver)
    • Silver – 2024 (Team Sprint - Women)

  • World Cup, 2023 (1000m - Women, 500m - Women, 1500m - Women)
Olympic Experience

Quick Facts
  • Ali is the daughter of Julie and Kevin Watson
  • She has one younger sibling, Bailey
  • She started diving in 2012 at age 11 (although she says she tried diving when she was 4 and freaked out)
  • Ali trains four hours a day, six days a week
  • Ali enjoys playing with her dogs, working out and playing tennis with her friends and family when she has free time. She is interested in science, specifically biology, neurology and neurosurgery
  • She would like to be a veterinarian one day
  • She also enjoys watching baseball (especially the New York Yankess) and would love to meet Derek Jeter
  • Outside of diving, Ali volunteers at the Humane Society of Broward County
  • She hopes to become a neurosurgeon
  • She has been named to the Academic Honor Roll (2015-2019. Ali was also a member of the 2019-2019 National English Honor Society and National Spanish Honor Society
  • Between dives, she listens to music, plays games on her phone and visualizes her next dive
  • Ali typically listens to upbeat music, sometimes rap. She also listens to songs that help her with confidence
  • Her favorite dive is a 5253B on 10-meter
  • Pushing herself after a long week of school and training, especially when she is sore, is the hardest part about training
  • Before competition, Ali usually eats a lot of protein and some form of energy chews. She also consumers a lot of water she competing
  • Her favorite meal outside of competition is chicken and rice
  • If she could play any other sport it would be tennis because both her parents played
  • Her favorite diver is Abby Johnston
  • Her favorite t.v. show is Grey's Anatomy

bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Son of Gerry and Molly Marie Watson
  • Has one sister, Melinda Watson Conner
  • Married wife Angie, a former basketball player for the University of Georgia and the WNBA, in 2004
  • Adopted son Caleb on March 26, 2012 and adopted daughter Dakota in December 2014
  • Formed the Bubba Watson Foundation in January 2014 with the mission to enhance the everyday lives of people in need and place a particular emphasis on helping and inspiring children, young adults and those associated with the U.S. military
